scenario: Trump declared Iran hostilities 'terminated' (May 1) to bypass War Powers 60-day deadline - legal maneuver only; blockade intact; nuclear stalemate. Iran phased Hormuz proposal rejected: Trump 'not satisfied', 'blast them away'. Brent $108.10 May 1 (-2.1%, TradingEconomics; hit $126 wartime high Apr 30 CNBC). TTF €46.15 (+0.35%); JKM confirmed $16.48 Apr 28 (-0.45%). Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; Hormuz ~13 transits/day; tail risk 45%.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases slightly (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1 -2.1%); Trump 'terminated' declaration is legal bypass only - blockade and Cape routing cost premium intact. Fertilizer supply disruption mechanism unchanged. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation softens with Brent $108.10 May 1 (-2.1%, TradingEconomics). Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; blockade and P&I suspension intact; fertilizer and freight disruption mechanism unchanged.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs ease with Brent $108.10 May 1 (-2.1%); Trump 'terminated' declaration is legal bypass; Cape routing costs elevated; structural supply chain disruption unresolved - blockade and P&I cancellation intact; tail 45%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO eases with Brent $108.10 May 1 (-2.1%); Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass only; tail 45%. Indonesia B40 mandate provides structural floor. Malaysia energy emergency (June supply uncertain, RON95 quota cut) adds domestic cost pressure. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting cycle unresolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure persists; Brent $108.10 May 1; Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass only; tail 45%. Q2 planting season urea availability risk unresolved; blockade intact; Qafco force majeure unresolved. Malaysia June supply gap risk remains.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha eases with Brent $108.10 May 1 (-2.1%, TradingEconomics); Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass - Gulf condensate interdiction unchanged; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Pengerang RAPID feedstock exposure remains elevated; tail 45%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene eases with naphtha on Brent $108.10 May 1 (-2.1%). Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; SABIC and QAPCO export bottlenecks persist - blockade intact; vessel interdiction not lifted; tail 45%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eases with crude complex (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1). Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure unresolved; blockade intact. China MTO complexes: feedstock pressure elevated. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases slightly (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1 -2.1%); Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; blockade intact. Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; US sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Tail 45%. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases slightly (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1 -2.1%); Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; Saudi Ma'aden shipping still disrupted; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade intact. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam remains acute; tail 45%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs ease slightly (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1 -2.1%). Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP - freight remains main transmission. Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; Cape rerouting costs persist; palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia face moderately elevated input costs.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI eases slightly (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1 -2.1%); Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; blockade and P&I suspension persist. Normalization still distant: mine-clearing 6 months even on deal. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates ease (May 2 est.; Brent $108.10 May 1 -2.1%); Trump 'terminated' declaration mild signal; blockade intact, P&I suspension unresolved, Hormuz ~13 transits/day; Iran holds 3 Western vessels. Tail (45%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI carried (weekly; last published Friday May 1). Trump 'terminated' declaration legal bypass; Cape rerouting premium persists - blockade intact, P&I unresolved. MSC Francesca still seized; Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration; tail 45%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly).
